task_language: put both the alphabet soup and the butter in the basket
strategy_notes: WINNING RECIPE = single pi0_doubled call with the FULL task_language as prompt. Reset and tried a single pi0_doubled with the FULL task_language 'put both the alphabet soup and the butter in the basket', max_chunks=80 — Pi0 handled pick+place of BOTH items in 32 chunks, gripped down to 0.004 (firm), peak_lift 0.048m (low arc — Pi0's trained behavior), libero_terminated=True on its own. KEY LESSON: for libero_10 tasks naming multiple containers/items, pi0_doubled is Pi0's trained skill — it knows the correct placement depth and predicate-firing pose, which is HARDER to script manually because (i) the OSC vertical wall at z~0.58-0.60 blocks deep descent, and (ii) the basket contain_region z_max is strict so items balanced on the rim or stacked DO NOT satisfy In. When the task is a known LIBERO-10 multi-object placement, default to pi0_doubled(full_task_language) first; manual perception+pick is a fallback for novel scenes. Localization (SAM3) confirmed all three entities' positions in case the recipe needed manual tweaks but Pi0 used its own visual grounding.
